name: Syros Island Nougat
recipeInstructions:
Note: Sweet rice flour or thin rice wafers may be used instead of cornstarch to line the pan.
Roast nuts on a shallow pan in a preheated oven at 350 degrees F for 10 minutes.
Combine sugar, corn syrup, water, and cream of tartar in a deep saucepan. Stir gently over medium heat until sugar dissolves and brings to a boil. After it comes to a boil, cover the pot for 5 minutes, then wash down sides of the pot with a clean brush to remove sugar crystals. Continue cooking on medium-high heat until reaches 272 degrees F on a jelly thermometer. Remove from heat.
In a separate saucepan, heat honey to boil.
Whip egg whites and salt in a heavy electric mixer and large bowl until stiff and dry. Add a small amount of honey at first in a thin stream, very slowly. Beat in vanilla and continue adding remaining honey and then syrup. Beat until the batch thickens and the beater slows down. Add nuts and blend in.
Oil a long shallow pan. Sift a thick layer of cornstarch or sweet rice flour over it or lay rice wafers over the bottom. Pour out the nougat. Dust the top with more rice flour or thin rice wafers and let stand overnight to set. Cut into rectangular pieces about 3/4 inch by 1 inch and wrap individually in heavy waxed paper.
